blocks.text: We would like to refer our patient over for Skilled nursing for wound care. Please see attached order and let us
know If you have any questions or concerns.
Malignant neoplasm of dome of bladder
History of present illness: Patient reports drainage from her lower midline incision in 2 areas. Patient was seen at the
emergency room and was placed on antibiotics. No imaging has been done. Patient undergoing immunotherapy as
per medical oncology for her metastatic squamous cell carcinoma of the bladder. Tolerating diet and colostomy is
Abdomen soft nontender nondistended. Colostomy functioning. Area of midline incision with 1 area draining
purulent material, no succus. This was probed with a Q-tip and noted to go into approximately 8 to 9 cm. Wound
Status post exam under anesthesia, diagnostic laparoscopy, robotic mobilization of left colon with sigmoid colon
with robotic lyse adhesions, exploratory laparotomy with takedown of colovesical and enterovesical fistulas and
segmental small bowel resection, repair of bladder dome and biopsy of bladder proximal diverging loop sigmoid
colostomy in a patient with squamous cell carcinoma of the bladder with invasion to the small bowel with metastatic
Discussed findings with both the patient and patient's son at length. Continue local wound care daily at home with
plain gauze packing. Will get CT scan of the abdomen pelvis with contrast to evaluate this wound/possible fistula.
Return to clinic after the CT scan is completed. Instructions given
E-signed by Arthur Fusco on 04/06/2026 11:07AM PDT
This page was generated at 04/06/2026 11:07AM PDT
5670 W Flamingo Rd, Ste D, Las Vegas NV 89103
Patient Address: 945 Midnight View Ave, Henderson, NV
Malignant neoplasm of dome of bladder,
Primary Needs for Home Health (check all that apply):
Skilled nursing to perform daily Pack wound with iodoform gauze, ensuring loose
packing to fill the wound bed without overpacking. Cleanse wound with normal
saline. Wound measures approximately 10 cm depth X 3 cm width.
Cover with appropriate sterile secondary dressing (e.g., dry gauze or ABD pad)
Skilled Physical Therapy evaluation and treatment for patient with
abdominal surgical incision and functional mobility limitations
Frequency: 2 times per week for 4 weeks, then reassess.
When we call, we will also need to obtain the following information before seeing the
patient. If you have these available, feel free to fax along with this form.
Recent H&P or recent progress notes

Patient does have follow-up with urology. Patient will have a CT scan of the abdomen pelvis with
contrast/cystogram to evaluate for possible bladder leak postsurgery. If there is no leak from her bladder or her
Foley catheter and JP drain can be removed. Patient does have follow-up with urology also. Risk benefits
alternative therapy explained to both the patient and patient's son at length who appeared to understand all
these issues and agreed. Patient will need treatment for her squamous cell carcinoma of her bladder
CT scan was reviewed with both the patient and patient's son at length. Patient has follow-up with them
urology to decide about removing Foley catheter. Patient has follow-up with medical oncology due to her
immunotherapy for bladder carcinoma. Return to clinic in 3 to 4 weeks for follow-up. Instructions given
It was explained to the patient and patient's son that most likely she will need to keep her diverging loop
colostomy since she has a lot of bladder carcinoma noted at the time of surgery. Patient was cleared to start
back on her immunotherapy as per medical oncology. Instructions given. Return to clinic as needed
Patient reports noting food in her urine as well as blood. Patient will be scheduled for a CT scan of the
abdomen pelvis with contrast. Patient is following up with urology for possible cystoscopy. This is all explained
at length to both the patient and patient's son who appeared to understand all these issues. Patient will return
to the office after getting her CT scan performed
Discussed palliative care with patient's son at length. Discussed possible need for bilateral nephrostomy tubes
by IR. Discouraged any further surgical intervention due to disease progression. Return to clinic as needed.
Follow-up with urology and medical oncology
Discussed findings with both the patient and patient's son at length. Continue local wound care daily at home
with plain gauze packing. Will get CT scan of the abdomen pelvis with contrast to evaluate this wound/possible
fistula. Return to clinic after the CT scan is completed. Instructions given
